content:
{
  "title": "Chain-of-Intent Prompting for Enhanced Code Generation in Large Language Models",
  "problem": "Current code generation prompts often lack explicit clarification of user intent, leading to generated code that misses nuanced requirements or edge cases. This results in decreased functional correctness and developer trust in LLM-based code assistants.",
  "motivation": "As LLMs are increasingly deployed in programming tasks, ensuring generated code aligns with user intent and business logic is critical. Standard prompts often under-specify requirements, especially in complex scenarios. Incorporating intent clarification directly into prompting could bridge this gap and yield more robust code outputs.",
  "method": "We propose Chain-of-Intent Prompting (CoIP), a multi-step prompting approach where the LLM first paraphrases and disambiguates the coding task, explicitly eliciting underlying intentions and potential edge cases. The model then generates code based on this refined, intent-enriched specification. Our method leverages intermediate intent extraction as a prompt engineering step, using either self-reflection or follow-up clarifying questions before final code synthesis.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate CoIP on HumanEval, MBPP, and CodeContests datasets, comparing against standard direct prompting and chain-of-thought prompting baselines. Key metrics include functional correctness, code completeness, and alignment with user intent (judged by human annotators). Ablation studies will assess the impact of intermediate intent clarification steps."
}
